pending.body:
finding_summary: `find_existing_bead_project` can adopt an unrelated manual project that merely happens to use the bead's default-derived project ID, and `milestone run` will then execute that project as if it were bead-backed.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs
- src/cli/milestone.rs
- src/cli/run.rs
evidence:
- src/cli/project.rs:582-587 returns `Some(default_record.id)` whenever the default-named project exists and `task_source` is `None`.
- src/cli/milestone.rs:763-799 treats that result as an existing bead project and links it into the milestone controller.
- src/cli/run.rs:1041-1043 and 1123-1125 both short-circuit when `task_source` is missing, so the adopted project never participates in milestone lineage sync or controller reconciliation.
remediation_hint: Only adopt projects whose `task_source` matches the milestone/bead, or require an explicit recovery marker/migration path before reusing a default-named project. Otherwise surface a duplicate/needs-operator error instead of auto-adopting the project ID.

finding_summary: The new shared bead-ref canonicalizer treats any dotted short ref as already fully qualified, so legitimate milestone-local refs like `2.1` no longer match `ms-alpha.2.1`; `milestone next`, controller resume, and `create-from-bead` then reject or misclassify those beads.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs
- src/cli/milestone.rs
- src/cli/project.rs
- src/cli/run.rs
evidence:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:8-15 now returns any ref containing `.` unchanged instead of prefixing it with the active milestone.
- src/cli/project.rs:991-1036 and src/cli/milestone.rs:1011-1044 both treat dotted input as an exact ID, so `br show 2.1 --json` returning `ms-alpha.2.1` is rejected.
- src/cli/run.rs:962-981 uses the same matcher during controller resume and will report the bead missing when the controller persisted only the dotted short form.
remediation_hint: Distinguish foreign fully-qualified IDs from milestone-local dotted suffixes instead of using `contains('.')` as the split. Add regression coverage for dotted short bead IDs (for example `2.1` vs `ms-alpha.2.1`).

status_summary: running: final review restart round 6 -> Plan and Implement
